Bioburden Analysis Procedure for In-Process Samples
1. Purpose
This procedure defines the steps required to estimate microbial load in collected samples.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
QC analysts are responsible for performing the procedure as written.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Marker labels
- Incubator
- Membrane filters
- Agar plates
- Sterile saline
5. Procedure
5.1 Count visible colonies after the incubation period.
5.2 Filter the prepared sample through the designated membrane.
5.3 Document any atypical colony morphology in the record form.
5.4 Place the membrane onto the selected agar medium.
5.5 Prepare the required dilution series using sterile diluent.
5.6 Incubate the plates for 7 days under defined culture conditions.
5.7 Verify sample identity before initiating the bioburden workflow.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

Routine Endotoxin Analysis Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the method for bacterial endotoxin testing of product samples.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to routine testing activities in GMP environments.
3. Responsibilities
QC analysts are responsible for performing the procedure as written.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Reagent water
- Microplate reader
- Pipettes
- Endotoxin reagent vials
- Pyrogen-free tubes
5. Procedure
5.1 Add the designated reagent volume to each prepared tube.
5.2 Compare the measured value against the predefined acceptance criterion.
5.3 Document all calculated and observed values in the approved worksheet.
5.4 Label each reaction tube with the assigned sample identifier.
5.5 Confirm that all endotoxin test reagents are within their assigned expiry period.
5.6 Incubate the reaction mixture at 35°C for the required reaction period.
5.7 Prepare the sample dilution using pyrogen-free water.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

Sample Labeling and Storage Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure defines the requirements for sample receipt, transfer, and temporary
storage.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Temperature monitor
- Barcode labels
- Insulated transfer box
- Sample containers
- Receiving checklist
5. Procedure
5.1 Attach a barcode or manual label to the received sample.
5.2 Notify the responsible analyst that the sample is available for testing.
5.3 Store the sample at 2–8°C according to handling requirements.
5.4 Record receipt time, condition, and storage location in the log.
5.5 Confirm sample identity against the accompanying documentation.
5.6 Escalate any discrepancy in labeling or condition to the supervisor.
5.7 Inspect the sample container for integrity and labeling accuracy.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

Bioburden Sampling Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for routine bioburden analysis in QC.
2. Scope

3. Responsibilities
Laboratory personnel shall follow this procedure and document all relevant observations.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Sterile saline
- Dilution tubes
- Vacuum manifold
- Incubator
- Sterile sample bottles
5. Procedure
5.1 Filter the prepared sample through the designated membrane.
5.2 Calculate the microbial load per defined sample volume.
5.3 Verify sample identity before initiating the bioburden workflow.
5.4 Prepare the required dilution series using sterile diluent.
5.5 Count visible colonies after the incubation period.
5.6 Place the membrane onto the selected agar medium.
5.7 Document any atypical colony morphology in the record form.
6. Documentation
Completed records shall be reviewed and archived according to site documentation
procedures.

Routine Environmental Monitoring Procedure for Controlled Areas
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for viable and non-viable environmental monitoring.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
QC analysts are responsible for performing the procedure as written.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Sampling log sheet
- Contact plates
- Settle plates
- Sampling map
- Marker pen
5. Procedure
5.1 Transfer collected media to the incubator under controlled conditions.
5.2 Label all collected media with area, date, and sample point.
5.3 Escalate any excursion above the action limit to Quality Assurance.
5.4 Incubate samples according to the approved environmental monitoring plan.
5.5 Record microbial growth observations after incubation.
5.6 Collect surface samples using contact plates according to the site sampling map.
5.7 Verify the sampling plan and room classification before monitoring.
6. Documentation
Completed records shall be reviewed and archived according to site documentation
procedures.

Routine Endotoxin Analysis Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the method for bacterial endotoxin testing of product samples.
2. Scope

- Microplate reader
- Pipettes
- Reagent water
- Disposable tips
- Heating block
5. Procedure
5.1 Incubate the reaction mixture at 37°C for the required reaction period.
5.2 Label each reaction tube with the assigned sample identifier.
5.3 Add the designated reagent volume to each prepared tube.
5.4 Mix the prepared solution for 60 seconds.
5.5 Confirm that all endotoxin test reagents are within their assigned expiry period.
5.6 Document all calculated and observed values in the approved worksheet.
5.7 Measure the response using the designated instrument.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

Mobile Phase Preparation Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the preparation of laboratory buffer solutions for routine
analytical use.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
Laboratory personnel shall follow this procedure and document all relevant observations.
4. Materials and Equipment
- pH meter
- Purified water
- Magnetic stirrer
- Analytical balance
- Beaker
5. Procedure
5.1 Transfer the prepared solution into the assigned storage container.
5.2 Adjust the solution to pH 7.2 using acid or base as required.
5.3 Add the measured material to 1000 mL of purified water.
5.4 Label the container with solution name, preparation date, and expiry.
5.5 Verify the identity and status of all required raw materials before preparation.
5.6 Mix the solution at 150 rpm until the material is dissolved.
5.7 Store the prepared buffer at 15–25°C unless otherwise specified.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

Acetate Buffer Preparation Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the preparation of laboratory buffer solutions for routine
analytical use.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to routine testing activities in GMP environments.
3. Responsibilities
Assigned analysts are responsible for execution, while supervisors are responsible for
review.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Purified water
- Magnetic stirrer
- Beaker
- Analytical balance
- Label printer
5. Procedure
5.1 Add the measured material to 250 mL of purified water.
5.2 Document preparation details in the approved preparation record.
5.3 Measure 10.0 g of the designated buffer component.
5.4 Verify the identity and status of all required raw materials before preparation.
5.5 Mix the solution at 150 rpm until the material is dissolved.
5.6 Transfer the prepared solution into the assigned storage container.
5.7 Adjust the solution to pH 5.5 using acid or base as required.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

Sterility Testing Procedure for Aseptic QC Samples
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for evaluating sterility test samples under
controlled conditions.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
Laboratory personnel shall follow this procedure and document all relevant observations.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Growth medium bottles
- Incubator
- Sterility test canisters
- Laminar flow hood
- Membrane filtration unit
5. Procedure
5.1 Transfer the sample aseptically into the test setup.
5.2 Incubate the test units for 10 days at the designated temperature range.
5.3 Verify that the sterility test area has been cleaned and released for use.
5.4 Rinse the membrane using sterile fluid as specified in the test plan.
5.5 Transfer the membrane into the assigned growth medium.
5.6 Filter the sample using the designated sterile membrane system.
5.7 Record the incubation start time, end time, and all observations.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

Analytical Balance Calibration Check Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the calibration workflow for selected laboratory instruments.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Reference weight set
- Temperature probe
- Cleaning wipes
- Instrument label
- Adjustment tool
5. Procedure
5.1 Prepare the required standard or reference material.
5.2 Document the calibration outcome in the approved equipment record.
5.3 Apply a calibration status label after completion of the activity.
5.4 Escalate any out-of-tolerance condition according to site procedure.
5.5 Record observed values at each calibration point.
5.6 Ensure the instrument is clean and ready for calibration activity.
5.7 Verify that all results fall within the defined tolerance of ±0.5.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

HPLC System Use Procedure for QC Testing

- Mobile phase bottles
- Integration software
- Analytical column
- HPLC system
5. Procedure

5.6 Prepare the mobile phase according to the approved composition.

This procedure describes the method for bioburden testing of in-process or bulk samples.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Agar plates
- Membrane filters
- Sterile sample bottles
- Vacuum manifold
- Incubator
5. Procedure
5.1 Document any atypical colony morphology in the record form.
5.2 Verify sample identity before initiating the bioburden workflow.
5.3 Filter the prepared sample through the designated membrane.
5.4 Incubate the plates for 7 days under defined culture conditions.
5.5 Place the membrane onto the selected agar medium.
5.6 Transfer the assigned sample volume to the test vessel.
5.7 Prepare the required dilution series using sterile diluent.
6. Documentation
Completed records shall be reviewed and archived according to site documentation
procedures.

Bacterial Endotoxin Testing Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for endotoxin test preparation and result recording.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
Laboratory personnel shall follow this procedure and document all relevant observations.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Heating block
- Pipettes
- Vortex mixer
- Endotoxin reagent vials
- Reagent water
5. Procedure
5.1 Mix the prepared solution for 45 seconds.
5.2 Compare the measured value against the predefined acceptance criterion.
5.3 Prepare the sample dilution using pyrogen-free water.
5.4 Incubate the reaction mixture at 35°C for the required reaction period.
5.5 Document all calculated and observed values in the approved worksheet.
5.6 Confirm that all endotoxin test reagents are within their assigned expiry period.
5.7 Label each reaction tube with the assigned sample identifier.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

Bioburden Analysis Procedure for In-Process Samples
1. Purpose
This procedure defines the steps required to estimate microbial load in collected samples.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Agar plates
- Sterile saline
- Marker labels
- Sterile sample bottles
- Incubator
5. Procedure
5.1 Prepare the required dilution series using sterile diluent.
5.2 Filter the prepared sample through the designated membrane.
5.3 Count visible colonies after the incubation period.
5.4 Calculate the microbial load per defined sample volume.
5.5 Verify sample identity before initiating the bioburden workflow.
5.6 Incubate the plates for 5 days under defined culture conditions.
5.7 Document any atypical colony morphology in the record form.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

QC Sample Transfer Procedure
1. Purpose

2. Scope
This procedure applies to routine testing activities in GMP environments.
3. Responsibilities
Laboratory personnel shall follow this procedure and document all relevant observations.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Storage rack
- Sample containers
- Sample logbook
- Receiving checklist
- Temperature monitor
5. Procedure
5.1 Transfer the sample to the designated storage area within 45 minutes.
5.2 Notify the responsible analyst that the sample is available for testing.

5.6 Attach a barcode or manual label to the received sample.
5.7 Store the sample at 2–8°C according to handling requirements.
6. Documentation
All observations shall be documented immediately after completion of the task.

Environmental Monitoring Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure defines the method for routine monitoring of controlled production and QC
environments.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
QC analysts are responsible for performing the procedure as written.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Sampling log sheet
- Alcohol wipes
- Active air sampler
- Marker pen
- Incubator
5. Procedure
5.1 Collect surface samples using contact plates according to the site sampling map.
5.2 Record microbial growth observations after incubation.
5.3 Escalate any excursion above the action limit to Quality Assurance.
5.4 Transfer collected media to the incubator under controlled conditions.
5.5 Verify the sampling plan and room classification before monitoring.
5.6 Perform active air sampling for 4 minutes at the required location.
5.7 Incubate samples according to the approved environmental monitoring plan.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

Routine HPLC Analysis Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for routine HPLC testing in regulated laboratory
work.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Analytical column
- Autosampler vials
- Integration software
- Mobile phase bottles
- Syringe filters
5. Procedure
5.1 Run the chromatographic method using the approved sequence table.
5.2 Set the column temperature to 35°C.
5.3 Prepare the mobile phase according to the approved composition.
5.4 Transfer the filtered sample into a labeled autosampler vial.
5.5 Review peak shape, retention time, and integration suitability.
5.6 Filter the sample using a 0.45 µm syringe filter.
5.7 Verify instrument status and ensure that calibration is current before use.
6. Documentation
Completed records shall be reviewed and archived according to site documentation
procedures.

Routine Sterility Testing Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for evaluating sterility test samples under
controlled conditions.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Laminar flow hood
- Membrane filtration unit
- Growth medium bottles
- Incubator
- Sterile sample containers
5. Procedure
5.1 Incubate the test units for 3 days at the designated temperature range.
5.2 Transfer the membrane into the assigned growth medium.
5.3 Inspect the units daily for evidence of microbial growth.
5.4 Escalate any suspected contamination event to the supervisor immediately.
5.5 Rinse the membrane using sterile fluid as specified in the test plan.
5.6 Filter the sample using the designated sterile membrane system.
5.7 Transfer the sample aseptically into the test setup.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

Wash Buffer Preparation Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the preparation of laboratory buffer solutions for routine
analytical use.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
Laboratory personnel shall follow this procedure and document all relevant observations.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Label printer
- Beaker
- Buffer salt
- Magnetic stirrer
- Purified water
5. Procedure
5.1 Mix the solution at 150 rpm until the material is dissolved.
5.2 Adjust the solution to pH 7.2 using acid or base as required.
5.3 Measure 4.0 g of the designated buffer component.
5.4 Add the measured material to 1000 mL of purified water.
5.5 Verify the identity and status of all required raw materials before preparation.
5.6 Document preparation details in the approved preparation record.
5.7 Label the container with solution name, preparation date, and expiry.
6. Documentation
Completed records shall be reviewed and archived according to site documentation
procedures.

Citrate Buffer Preparation Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the preparation of laboratory buffer solutions for routine
analytical use.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
Assigned analysts are responsible for execution, while supervisors are responsible for
review.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Label printer
- Beaker
- Magnetic stirrer
- pH meter
- Analytical balance
5. Procedure
5.1 Measure 12.5 g of the designated buffer component.
5.2 Adjust the solution to pH 7.0 using acid or base as required.
5.3 Store the prepared buffer at 2–8°C unless otherwise specified.
5.4 Label the container with solution name, preparation date, and expiry.
5.5 Mix the solution at 250 rpm until the material is dissolved.
5.6 Transfer the prepared solution into the assigned storage container.
5.7 Add the measured material to 2000 mL of purified water.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

analytical use.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to personnel assigned to controlled analytical operations.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Label printer
- Analytical balance
- Graduated cylinder
- Buffer salt
- Magnetic stirrer
5. Procedure
5.1 Store the prepared buffer at 15–25°C unless otherwise specified.
5.2 Transfer the prepared solution into the assigned storage container.
5.3 Document preparation details in the approved preparation record.
5.4 Measure 4.0 g of the designated buffer component.
5.5 Adjust the solution to pH 6.8 using acid or base as required.
5.6 Label the container with solution name, preparation date, and expiry.
5.7 Add the measured material to 1000 mL of purified water.
6. Documentation
Completed records shall be reviewed and archived according to site documentation
procedures.

HPLC System Use Procedure for QC Testing
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for routine HPLC testing in regulated laboratory
work.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
QC analysts are responsible for performing the procedure as written.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Waste bottle
- Syringe filters
- HPLC system
- Mobile phase bottles
- Pipettes
5. Procedure
5.1 Verify instrument status and ensure that calibration is current before use.
5.2 Transfer the filtered sample into a labeled autosampler vial.
5.3 Prepare the mobile phase according to the approved composition.
5.4 Document all observations and calculated values in the approved worksheet.
5.5 Run the chromatographic method using the approved sequence table.
5.6 Set the column temperature to 40°C.
5.7 Compare the result against the predefined system suitability criteria.
6. Documentation
All observations shall be documented immediately after completion of the task.

Routine Sterility Testing Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for evaluating sterility test samples under
controlled conditions.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
Laboratory personnel shall follow this procedure and document all relevant observations.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Growth medium bottles
- Sterile forceps
- Incubator
- Sterile sample containers
- Sterility test canisters
5. Procedure
5.1 Rinse the membrane using sterile fluid as specified in the test plan.
5.2 Verify that the sterility test area has been cleaned and released for use.
5.3 Inspect the units daily for evidence of microbial growth.
5.4 Escalate any suspected contamination event to the supervisor immediately.
5.5 Filter the sample using the designated sterile membrane system.
5.6 Incubate the test units for 5 days at the designated temperature range.
5.7 Record the incubation start time, end time, and all observations.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

Bioburden Analysis Procedure for In-Process Samples
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the method for bioburden testing of in-process or bulk samples.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
QC analysts are responsible for performing the procedure as written.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Agar plates
- Incubator
- Sterile saline
- Marker labels
- Sterile sample bottles
5. Procedure
5.1 Incubate the plates for 14 days under defined culture conditions.
5.2 Transfer the assigned sample volume to the test vessel.
5.3 Count visible colonies after the incubation period.
5.4 Verify sample identity before initiating the bioburden workflow.
5.5 Prepare the required dilution series using sterile diluent.
5.6 Place the membrane onto the selected agar medium.
5.7 Calculate the microbial load per defined sample volume.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

Endotoxin Screening Procedure for Product Samples
1. Purpose
This procedure defines the workflow for endotoxin analysis using validated laboratory
practices.

This procedure applies to analysts performing regulated laboratory tasks.
3. Responsibilities
QC analysts are responsible for performing the procedure as written.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Pipettes
- Disposable tips
- Pyrogen-free tubes
- Heating block
- Vortex mixer
5. Procedure
5.1 Mix the prepared solution for 10 seconds.

5.5 Confirm that all endotoxin test reagents are within their assigned expiry period.
5.6 Compare the measured value against the predefined acceptance criterion.
5.7 Incubate the reaction mixture at 35°C for the required reaction period.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

Sample Receipt and Handling Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure describes the handling, labeling, and storage of incoming QC samples.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to QC laboratory personnel.
3. Responsibilities
QC analysts are responsible for performing the procedure as written.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Barcode labels
- Insulated transfer box
- Storage rack
- Sample containers
- Temperature monitor
5. Procedure
5.1 Transfer the sample to the designated storage area within 45 minutes.
5.2 Confirm sample identity against the accompanying documentation.
5.3 Assign the sample tracking number using the approved log system.
5.4 Inspect the sample container for integrity and labeling accuracy.
5.5 Store the sample at 2–8°C according to handling requirements.
5.6 Notify the responsible analyst that the sample is available for testing.
5.7 Attach a barcode or manual label to the received sample.
6. Documentation
All generated data shall be reviewed for completeness and retained in the approved
archive.

Chromatographic Sample Preparation and Analysis Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure defines the method for chromatographic sample preparation and system
operation.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to routine testing activities in GMP environments.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Syringe filters
- Mobile phase bottles
- Analytical column
- Waste bottle
- Pipettes
5. Procedure
5.1 Transfer the filtered sample into a labeled autosampler vial.
5.2 Verify instrument status and ensure that calibration is current before use.
5.3 Document all observations and calculated values in the approved worksheet.
5.4 Filter the sample using a 0.2 µm syringe filter.
5.5 Review peak shape, retention time, and integration suitability.
5.6 Set the column temperature to 25°C.
5.7 Compare the result against the predefined system suitability criteria.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

Microbiological Environmental Monitoring Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for viable and non-viable environmental monitoring.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to routine testing activities in GMP environments.
3. Responsibilities
Assigned analysts are responsible for execution, while supervisors are responsible for
review.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Active air sampler
- Settle plates
- Incubator
- Alcohol wipes
- Contact plates
5. Procedure
5.1 Incubate samples according to the approved environmental monitoring plan.
5.2 Record microbial growth observations after incubation.
5.3 Place the settle plates at the designated monitoring locations.
5.4 Verify the sampling plan and room classification before monitoring.
5.5 Transfer collected media to the incubator under controlled conditions.
5.6 Escalate any excursion above the action limit to Quality Assurance.
5.7 Perform active air sampling for 10 minutes at the required location.
6. Documentation
All results shall be recorded in the controlled laboratory record form.

Phosphate Buffer Preparation Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure defines the steps for preparing and labeling synthetic buffer solutions.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to routine testing activities in GMP environments.
3. Responsibilities
Assigned analysts are responsible for execution, while supervisors are responsible for
review.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Analytical balance
- Label printer
- Purified water
- Buffer salt
- pH meter
5. Procedure
5.1 Mix the solution at 400 rpm until the material is dissolved.
5.2 Label the container with solution name, preparation date, and expiry.
5.3 Measure 10.0 g of the designated buffer component.
5.4 Store the prepared buffer at 15–25°C unless otherwise specified.
5.5 Add the measured material to 500 mL of purified water.
5.6 Document preparation details in the approved preparation record.
5.7 Adjust the solution to pH 7.0 using acid or base as required.
6. Documentation
All observations shall be documented immediately after completion of the task.

Routine Sterility Testing Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure provides instructions for evaluating sterility test samples under
controlled conditions.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to personnel assigned to controlled analytical operations.
3. Responsibilities
Assigned analysts are responsible for execution, while supervisors are responsible for
review.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Membrane filtration unit
- Laminar flow hood
- Growth medium bottles
- Alcohol spray
- Incubator
5. Procedure
5.1 Verify that the sterility test area has been cleaned and released for use.
5.2 Inspect the units daily for evidence of microbial growth.
5.3 Escalate any suspected contamination event to the supervisor immediately.
5.4 Transfer the sample aseptically into the test setup.
5.5 Record the incubation start time, end time, and all observations.
5.6 Incubate the test units for 3 days at the designated temperature range.
5.7 Transfer the membrane into the assigned growth medium.
6. Documentation
All observations shall be documented immediately after completion of the task.

1. Purpose

controlled conditions.

This procedure applies to routine testing activities in GMP environments.

5.4 Record the incubation start time, end time, and all observations.

5.6 Verify that the sterility test area has been cleaned and released for use.
5.7 Transfer the membrane into the assigned growth medium.

Bioburden Sampling Procedure
1. Purpose
This procedure defines the steps required to estimate microbial load in collected samples.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to routine testing activities in GMP environments.
3. Responsibilities
Supervisors are responsible for reviewing completed records and confirming compliance.
4. Materials and Equipment
- Membrane filters
- Marker labels
- Incubator
- Dilution tubes
- Agar plates
5. Procedure
5.1 Place the membrane onto the selected agar medium.
5.2 Verify sample identity before initiating the bioburden workflow.
5.3 Incubate the plates for 10 days under defined culture conditions.
5.4 Count visible colonies after the incubation period.
5.5 Prepare the required dilution series using sterile diluent.
5.6 Calculate the microbial load per defined sample volume.
5.7 Transfer the assigned sample volume to the test vessel.
6. Documentation
All observations shall be documented immediately after completion of the task.
